Last time we played around with some fun ways to pull values out of a dict. This time I want to keep going with dictionaries — keys, values, items — and then take a detour into tuples, since they show up the moment you call items(). Plus a quick look at Python's in operator and how it compares to JS.

Learning Python From a JS Angle (9) — Container Types, Part 1
iThome Ironman Contest

How did the BMI homework go? Whether you finished it or not, time waits for no one, so let's move on to the next topic. This time we dig into Python's container types — lists and dictionaries — and how they differ from JavaScript's arrays and objects. We'll also cover the fun stuff like get() and setdefault().

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (8) - BMI Calculator (1)

Time to harvest what we've covered so far with a little exercise. I like to drop a small practice project every few chapters so this series isn't just reading about Python — building something tiny makes it stick. This time it's the classic BMI calculator, written first in JavaScript and then in Python, plus a look at Python's string formatting.

Learn Python From a JavaScript Angle (7) — Conditionals and Loops

第 13 屆 iT邦幫忙鐵人賽

Next up: conditionals and loops, since you basically can't write any program without these two. We'll line up JavaScript's if/else against Python's if/elif/else, talk about the new match statement in Python 3.10, and cover for and while loops including how to fake a do...while in Python.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (6) - Variable Scope
iThome Ironman Contest 13th

Last time we covered functions and variables, so now I want to circle back and fill in something I skipped: variable scope. We'll look at how Python's scoping differs from JavaScript's var/let/const, why Python behaves a lot like var, whether Python has a scope chain, and what nonlocal and global are actually for.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (5) - Functions
Learn Python From JavaScript

Last time we talked about types and variables, so this time there's no dodging functions. Functions are a real sticking point for a lot of beginners, so I'll share a simple way to think about when you should reach for a function, plus how to practice writing them — all coming at it from a JavaScript angle.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (4) - Types and Variables

Okay, we're finally writing some actual Python. We've messed around for three days now, and if I don't start soon people are going to unsubscribe. Before we write anything, we kick off with the most basic thing there is: types. And as always, I'll compare Python and JavaScript side by side so the differences are easy to spot.

Homebrew

Next up is actually installing Python — because if we want to learn Python, we kind of need Python on our machine first. The install looks trivial, but Windows and macOS each have their own quirks and a couple of gotchas, so I'm giving both of them their own walkthrough here.

Learning Python From a JavaScript Background (2): The Origin Story
Learning Python From a JavaScript Background

Every programming language has its own little birth story. JavaScript's is famously chaotic (the whole "Java vs JavaScript is like dog vs hot dog" thing), so what about Python? Is its history just as fun? This post takes a quick look at where Python came from, why it's named after a comedy troupe, and what makes its syntax so clean.

Learn Python From a JavaScript Dev's Angle (1) - Table of Contents and Rambling

This is my third Ironman challenge this year and my fourth Ironman series overall. Honestly I'm a little scared I won't finish this time, because this series is pretty much learning Python from scratch — but from a JavaScript developer's point of view. Here's the table of contents and a bit of rambling to kick things off.
